name: Pasta with Sauce Veracruz
recipeIngredient: 4 to 6 fresh New Mexican green chiles
1 1/2 to 2 cups Rich chicken, beef or vegetable stock
2 tbsp Chopped fresh sweet marjoram or mild oregano
1 t Cumin seeds, toasted and coarsely ground
2 tsp Coriander seeds, toasted and coarsely ground
1/4 cup Tequila, reposado or anejo type
12 oz Ziti, rigatoni, fettuccine or linguine
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut zucchini into very thin strips (soften in hot water if dry packed).
2. Cook pasta according to package directions.
3. Wash fresh vegetables and pat dry. Rub with olive oil and place on a well-oiled baking sheet. Bake at 450°F until skins are browned and insides softened. Remove from oven when done; onions and garlic need 30-40 minutes.
4. While vegetables roast, heat stock to boiling, pour over sun-dried tomatoes, and let stand.
5. When vegetables are cool, squeeze garlic cloves, remove skin from onions and peppers, seed and core peppers. Roughly chop all vegetables.
6. Heat olive oil in a large skillet, add chopped vegetables and stir often. Add sun-dried tomatoes and soaking liquid, rosemary, sweet marjoram or oregano, parsley, cumin, coriander, capers, and tequila. Bring to boil, reduce heat, and simmer for 10-15 minutes until flavorful.
7. If needed, thin sauce with more stock. Add vanilla extract and sliced zucchini; cook just until bright. Season with salt and pepper.
8. Serve sauce over pasta, tossing lightly; garnish with cilantro, nuts, and grated cheese.
Yield: 6 to 8 servings.
